name: Panama Canal
climate: Tropical maritime; hot and humid year-round; pronounced wet season (May-December); annual rainfall ~2500mm; El Nino-sensitive

neighbors.name: NAFTA/USMCA
neighbors.relevance: US and Mexico are top canal users; Gulf Coast ports feed Atlantic transit

neighbors.name: Mercosur
neighbors.relevance: South American east coast trade relies on canal for Pacific access

neighbors.name: Cape of Good Hope
neighbors.relevance: Alternative route when canal faces restrictions or congestion

neighbors.name: Suez Canal
neighbors.relevance: Complementary global chokepoint; disruption to either redirects traffic

links.description: Twin global canal chokepoints; disruption spillover affects container rates worldwide
US Gulf-to-Asia trade and grain exports transit the canal heavily
Fallback route for oversized vessels and during drought-driven transit restrictions
Combined chokepoint chain for Asia-US East Coast container trade
US Midwest grain exports to Asia transit via Gulf ports through the canal
Canal links Atlantic economies to Indo-Pacific markets
